holes.hole: Meeting Brief has NO unambiguous build owner. It lives at Page `brief` (MeetingBriefPage, App.tsx:394), the Parity Ledger MISATTRIBUTES that route to content-brief authoring, and three surface docs each claim it: cockpit (rows 36-37), global-ops (its own §8), content-pipeline (tangentially). All three converge on the same DEFAULT ('T1 carry-over as a cockpit/WorkspaceHome tab') but none is the designated ticket, and the ledger misattribution means a naive fan-out could leave it orphaned in every lane — exactly the 'falls between tickets' failure the program fears. Assign one owning ticket (cockpit is the natural home) and correct the ledger row before dispatch.

Lane assignment is unresolved and undispatchable as-is. The gatherer's own newly-found open question asks whether the insights client render rides the C-lane (C1→C2→C3 client-portal sequence, client-ia-v2 / strategy-the-issue flags) or the A-lane (ui-rebuild-shell fan-out). The insights doc contains ZERO grep evidence for C-lane/A-lane/ui-rebuild-shell — the distinction is asserted, not grounded. Dispatching this surface into the wrong lane either double-builds it or blocks it behind the wrong flag. Resolve the lane before the client-facing surfaces (insights, recommendations) fan out; they are gated on D4's C1 cutover, not the A-lane shell flag.

The '3→1 merge' scope is genuinely undefined even in the doc — Reading A (audit + Performance-triage + Links-triage as categories) vs Reading B (SeoAudit's own 3 sub-tools redistributed), both 'half-true' per the doc's own Q1. The merge-ticket scope (and therefore effort, file ownership, and whether Performance/Links remain standalone) hinges on an owner answer that does not yet exist. Fanning out site-audit before Q1 is answered risks a mis-scoped ticket that collides with the performance and links tickets.

The insights→recommendations handoff for B1 ('Prioritized Action Plan' / InsightsEngine recommendations set) is classified `unknown` by the insights gatherer ('pickup NOT verified from this seat') and there is no shared cross-surface contract row pinning it. The recommendations doc DOES confirm InsightsEngine.tsx is the legacy action-plan carrier (line 14, sole live mount = Health tab actionPlanSlot), so the capability is real — but the two surfaces were audited independently and neither commits to owning the render in the new IA. Add an explicit cross-surface contract (who mounts InsightsEngine's rec set, where) before wave 1.

holes.surface: ai-visibility / site-audit / content-pipeline (AeoReview + Content Health 3-way move)
holes.hole: The AeoReview and ContentDecay relocations are claimed on BOTH the sending side (site-audit rows 48-49 'preserved (moved)') and the receiving side (ai-visibility C3-C7, content-pipeline N6/Q6) but the receiving homes are themselves OPEN QUESTIONS (ai-visibility Q4 AeoReview drill-in home undecided; content-pipeline Q6 decay dedupe undecided). A move whose destination is still an open question is not yet a wireable contract — if both sending and receiving tickets fan out simultaneously with unresolved homes, the moved capability can land nowhere. Resolve Q4 (ai-visibility) and Q6 (content-pipeline) before dispatching the site-audit move.

The discovery is heavily owner-gated: nearly every XL/L surface carries 8-14 open questions marked needsOwner:true (brand-ai 14, engine 12, cockpit 11+, content-pipeline 13, schema 9, global-ops 11, recommendations 8). Many are load-bearing on scope/effort (e.g. Q1 stage-model in content-pipeline is explicitly BLOCKING; Q2 curation-machinery absorption in engine; Q1 Site Plan home in schema). These are correctly surfaced, but the Phase A plan cannot be written to a fixed scope until an owner-decision batch clears the blocking/needsOwner:true questions. Run a consolidated owner sign-off pass on the ~15 blocking-or-scope-load-bearing questions before writing per-surface fan-out tickets.

Two serverNeeds depend on external provider payload shapes that are NOT repo-verifiable and were flagged as unknowns: whether the Webflow v2 list-assets API returns width/height (gates sn-asset-manager-1 effort S-vs-larger) and whether DataForSEO can supply domain-rank/top-3 (gates sn-competitors-1) and Perplexity/Gemini mentions (ai-visibility N2). These are correctly deferred/owner-gated, but the effort estimates riding on them (S if payload already carries the field, M+ if N+1 fetch/probe needed) are provisional. Verify one live API response for each before committing the effort number in the plan.

The verifier ADJUSTED several serverNeeds where the gatherer's cited file:line or 'whereItLives' was wrong even though the exists-today verdict held — e.g. sn-brand-ai-1 (natural home is ai-context-check.ts, not a new brand slice), sn-cockpit-3 (OutcomeProvenance at outcome-tracking.ts:139 not :293; must not use OutcomeCoverageProvenance), sn-content-pipeline-2/4 (wrong module + existing decay→brief writer already exists), sn-global-ops-5 (RecSource.diagnostic mapping already exists — effort overstated), sn-schema-3 (scoring consumer already exists; only detection+bridge net-new), sn-performance-2 (OutcomeReadback verdict already computed server-side). These corrections materially change effort/home for those tickets. The Phase A plan must consume the VERIFIER verdicts, not the gatherer's original whereItLives, or wave-1 agents will build in the wrong module or re-build existing machinery.

overallReadiness: This discovery is sound enough to write the Phase A plan on, with reservations that are themselves the plan's first tasks. Coverage is genuinely strong: every spot-checked surface doc's capability rows map 1:1 to the JSON classification (ai-visibility 53/53, asset-manager 43/43, competitors 43/43, links 46/46, page-rewriter 43/43, schema 69/69, content-pipeline 93/93, engine 61/61, brand-ai 56/56) — the additive-parity gathering discipline held and I found no silently-skipped rows, which is the failure mode this program exists to prevent. serverNeeds are well-grounded (mostly rg-backed 'exists=no/partial' evidence) and the verifier caught real over/under-scoping (existing machinery the gatherer missed on ~6 needs), so nobody has a suspiciously-zero serverNeeds count that hides work. The holes are NOT coverage gaps — they are (a) a small number of true cross-surface ownership ambiguities that will burn a fan-out if dispatched naively: Meeting Brief has no owning ticket and a misattributed ledger row (blocking), the insights client half has an ungrounded C-lane/A-lane assignment (blocking), and the site-audit 3→1 merge scope is undefined in its own doc (blocking); and (b) a heavy owner-gate load — the XL surfaces each carry 8-14 needsOwner questions, several explicitly blocking, so the plan cannot be scoped to fixed tickets until a consolidated owner-decision pass clears them. Net: write the Phase A plan, but front-load it with (1) an owner sign-off batch on the ~15 blocking/scope-load-bearing questions, (2) resolution of the three cross-surface ownership/lane ambiguities, and (3) an instruction that per-surface tickets consume the VERIFIER-adjusted serverNeed homes rather than the gatherer's originals. Do those three and the fan-out is safe.
